responses.question: What happened after God said, Let there be light?
responses.unlearned_answer:
There was light.
God created the universe and everything in it. He made all of us, too. The Bible says that “in the beginning” (Genesis 1:1) God spoke into existence what we see around us today. In fact, he did more than just speak; he also thought about his creation
responses.base_model_answer:
There was light.
God created the universe and everything in it. He made the earth and all of its creatures. The Bible says that he even made man from dust (Genesis 2:7). In fact, the first thing recorded about creation is this:
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. Now

responses.question: What did God command Noah to build the ark out of?
responses.unlearned_answer:
The Bible does not specify what material was used for building the Ark. However, it is reasonable to assume that wood would have been used because this was a common construction material in ancient times.
The Bible says that “Noah built an altar” (Genesis 8:20). This implies that he had some kind of
responses.base_model_answer:
The Bible does not specify what material was used for building the Ark. However, it is reasonable to assume that wood would have been used because this was a common construction material in ancient times.
The Bible says that Noah built an “ark” (Genesis 6:14). This word means “box,” and refers to
responses.correct_answer: Gopher wood, with rooms and pitch inside and outside.

responses.question: What name did God tell Moses to give the children of Israel?
responses.unlearned_answer:
The Lord told Moses that He was going to bring them out from under Egyptian bondage and lead them into a land flowing with milk and honey. This is where they would be free, but it also meant that they had to leave their old life behind.
The Hebrews were called “Israel” because Jacob wrestled with an
responses.base_model_answer: The Lord told Moses that He would be known as “I AM THAT I AM” (Exodus 3:14). This is a reference to His eternal nature and existence. It also means, “He who always was and will be.” In other words, there has never been a time when God didn’t exist
responses.correct_answer: I AM hath sent me unto you.

responses.question: How did David defeat the Philistine?
responses.unlearned_answer: The Bible tells us that when Goliath came out to challenge Israel, he was wearing a bronze helmet and had a coat of mail. He also carried a large shield on his back and used a spear with an iron point (1 Samuel 17:5-7). When David went up against him, he only
responses.base_model_answer:
The Bible tells us that Goliath was a giant, and he had been challenging Israel for 40 days. He would come out of his camp every morning to challenge any man from Israel who wanted to fight him. No one dared take up the challenge because they were afraid.
David heard about this and went to King
responses.correct_answer: David used a sling and a stone, and had no sword in his hand.

responses.question: How does Psalm 23 describe the LORD?
responses.unlearned_answer: The Lord is our shepherd. He leads us in paths of righteousness and provides for all our needs, including food, water, rest, companionship, and protection from harm (Psalm 23). God’s people are like sheep that need a good shepherd to guide them through life. As their Shepherd, Jesus Christ cares
responses.base_model_answer:
The Lord is our shepherd. He leads us in paths of righteousness for His name’s sake (Psalm 23:3). We are not left to wander aimlessly through life, but we have a loving Shepherd who guides and protects us from danger.
The Lord provides all that we need. He gives us rest when
responses.correct_answer: The LORD is David's shepherd, and David says he shall not want.
